title: ReplaySnapshot
description: Replay query and result schema for acquisition snapshots — allows re-running past acquisitions against current normalization or emitting historical results.

ReplayQuery.title: ReplayQuery
ReplayQuery.description: Parameters to identify and constrain the set of historical acquisitions to replay.

replay_id.description: Unique identifier for this replay operation.

start.description: Inclusive start of the replay time window.

end.description: Inclusive end of the replay time window.

time_range.description: Time window for the replay (ISO 8601 UTC).

source_ids.description: Optional filter: only replay acquisitions from these source contracts.

event_types.description: Optional filter: only replay acquisitions matching these event types.

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return.

replay_mode.description: Mode of replay: re-run normalization, re-fetch from source, or dry-run validation.

ReplayResult.title: ReplayResult
ReplayResult.description: The outcome of a single replay operation, including metadata, errors, and produced observations.

replay_id.description: Matches the ReplayQuery.replay_id this result corresponds to.

status.description: Overall status of the replay operation.

total_processed.description: Number of raw documents processed during replay.

observations_produced.description: Number of new or updated observations emitted.

errors.description: List of errors encountered during replay.

duration_ms.description: Wall-clock duration of the replay in milliseconds.

snapshot_id.description: If persisted, the ID of the snapshot that captured this result.
